truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against the true vulnerabilities in enhanced_calc.cpp. Strictly evaluates whether Run 2's claims were supported by its own output (not post-hoc discoveries).

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the hardcoded admin secret (SC-V3-010) with the exact quote matching the source (line 73). The CWE (798) and severity (Critical in truth, but Run 2 marked as Critical) align.

Run 2 identified the integer overflow in factorial (SC-V3-002) with the exact code snippet (lines 315–317). CWE (190) and severity (Medium in Run 2 vs. High in truth) are close; severity discrepancy is minor.
Run 2 identified the use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush (SC-V3-003) with the exact code (lines 813–827). CWE (416) and severity (High in Run 2 vs. Critical in truth) align closely.
Run 2 identified command injection in config validation (SC-V3-004) with the exact code (lines 563–568). CWE (78) and severity (Critical) match.

Run 2 identified a stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression (SC-V3-005) but misclassified the CWE as CWE-125 (Buffer Overflow) instead of CWE-120/CWE-121. The code quote matches the source (lines 830–832). Severity (High) matches.

Run 2 did not identify the race condition in ThreadPool::ThreadSafeCounter (SC-V3-006). The closest finding was a race condition in MemoryPool::cleanup (CWE-362), but that is a different vulnerability (SC-V3-003).

Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb in authentication (SC-V3-007). The emergency override was noted in the context of command injection (SC-V3-004's audit logging), but the logic bomb itself (authenticated_ = true after 5 failed attempts + EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE) was not flagged.

Run 2 did not identify the path traversal in configuration loading (SC-V3-008). The config loading was mentioned in other findings (e.g., command injection), but path traversal via config_dir_ + filename was not explicitly called out.

Run 2 identified a heap overflow in ExpressionParser::parse (SC-V3-009) with the exact code (lines 366–376). However, it classified the CWE as CWE-125 (Buffer Overflow) instead of CWE-122 (Heap-based Buffer Overflow). Severity (High) matches.
Run 2 identified hardcoded admin credentials (SC-V3-010) with the exact quote (line 73). CWE (798) and severity (Critical in Run 2 vs. High in truth) align closely.

Run 2 identified weak session token generation (SC-V3-011) with the exact code (lines 635–640). However, it classified the CWE as CWE-330 (Use of Insufficiently Random Values) instead of CWE-338 (Use of Predictable Algorithm). Severity (Low in Run 2 vs. High in truth) is a discrepancy.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
static std::tm cached_time;
if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the thread-unsafe use of localtime (SC-V3-012) with the exact code (lines 230–231). However, it classified the CWE as CWE-366 (Race Condition in Access of Shared Resource) instead of CWE-362 (Race Condition). Severity (Medium) matches.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; (Windows)
std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; (Unix)
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified insecure temporary file handling (SC-V3-013) with the exact code (lines 622–633). CWE (377) and severity (Low in Run 2 vs. Medium in truth) align closely.

Run 2 did not identify the persistent authentication state vulnerability (SC-V3-014). While it mentioned authenticated_ and session_token_, it did not flag the lack of session invalidation as a vulnerability.

Run 2 identified missing input validation in handle_variable_command (SC-V3-015) with the exact code (line 729). However, it classified the CWE as CWE-20 (Improper Input Validation) instead of CWE-754 (Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions). Severity (Medium in Run 2 vs. Low in truth) is a minor discrepancy.
Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in MemoryPool::Block split (SC-V3-016). The memory pool was analyzed, but the specific underflow in SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES (line 133) was not flagged.
Run 2 identified unbounded recursion in the parser (SC-V3-017) with the exact code (lines 381–433). CWE (674) and severity (Medium in Run 2 vs. High in truth) align closely.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in result_cache_ (SC-V3-018). While it mentioned result_cache_, it did not flag the thread-safety issue explicitly.
Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU in config loading (SC-V3-019). The config loading was analyzed, but the time-of-check-to-time-of-use (exists + sleep + ifstream) was not flagged.
Run 2 did not identify the integer division by zero in calculate_route_score (SC-V3-020). The function was not analyzed for this specific issue.
